description: Record-signature algorithm registry, keyed by COSE algorithm label as it appears in a COSE_Sign1 protected header (the integer labels live in the IANA COSE Algorithms registry; this registry pins which of them Label 309 accepts). Record-level signatures are ALWAYS OPTIONAL in Label 309: authorship is an opt-in claim, never required for a record to verify. A signature's COSE protected `alg` MUST name a registered label; any other label surfaces as SIGNATURE_UNSUPPORTED at info severity and does not fail the record. Verification is strict, non-cofactored Ed25519 per RFC 8032 §5.1.7 regardless of which registered label is used. Reserved labels — -7 (ES256, RFC 9053 §2.1), -49 (ML-DSA-65, RFC 9964 / FIPS 204), and the hash-based slh-dsa-sha2-128s (FIPS 205) — are not registered under v1 and surface as SIGNATURE_UNSUPPORTED; the record's content claim remains valid. Additions are additive-only and must ship with conformance vectors.

entries.description: EdDSA, the curve-agnostic COSE label, pinned to Ed25519 by Label 309. The MANDATORY baseline label every conforming verifier MUST accept, and the only `alg` CIP-30 wallets emit on the signData path. IANA marks -8 deprecated as of RFC 9864 in favour of fully-specified codepoints, but v1 keeps it MANDATORY because the curve ambiguity the deprecation targets does not apply: the curve is pinned to Ed25519 at two layers — the COSE_Key carries kty = 1 (OKP) and crv = 6 (Ed25519), and the path-1 32-byte protected-header kid convention is unambiguously an Ed25519 public key.
entries.reference: RFC 9053 §2.2 (CBOR Object Signing and Encryption — EdDSA) and RFC 8032 (Edwards-Curve Digital Signature Algorithm, Ed25519)

entries.description: Ed25519, the fully-specified COSE label registered alongside -8. OPT-INFO: a producer MAY emit it and a verifier MAY implement it. A verifier that implements it verifies it identically to the -8 baseline under the same strict non-cofactored Ed25519 primitive; one that does not surfaces the affected `sigs[i]` as SIGNATURE_UNSUPPORTED (info) and the record's content claim still validates.
entries.reference: RFC 8032 §5.1 (Edwards-Curve Digital Signature Algorithm, Ed25519) and RFC 9864 §2.2 (IANA assignment of the fully-specified -19 codepoint)
